thread.title: AAP Clinches Three Punjab Bypolls; Congress Snags Barnala Upset | Chandigarh News - Times of India
thread.title_full: AAP Clinches Three Punjab Bypolls; Congress Snags Barnala Upset | Chandigarh News - Times of India

author: Vibhor Mohan
title: AAP Clinches Three Punjab Bypolls; Congress Snags Barnala Upset | Chandigarh News - Times of India
text: Chandigarh: The Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) on Saturday managed to clinch three of the four Punjab assembly bypolls in Giddarbaha, Dera Baba Nanak and Chabbewal, where the Congress had previously won in 2022, but lost its own stronghold in Barnala where rebel factor did it in, making the sole seat a proverbial silver lining for the opposition Congress.In a verdict that would come as a morale booster for chief minister Bhagwant Mann, his party emerged victorious in the hotly contested seat of Giddarbaha, managed to make inroads in the Majha region with Dera Baba Nanak and registered a decisive win in Chabbewal.We also published the following articles recentlyPunjab bypoll results: AAP wins 3 seats, loses from its stronghold to CongressPunjab's ruling Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) secured victory in three out of four assembly constituencies during the recent by-elections. The contests were triggered by four sitting MLAs winning parliamentary seats earlier this year. AAP's success in Chabbewal, Dera Baba Nanak, and Gidderbaha, while Congress won Barnala, is seen as a significant endorsement of AAP's governance in the state.115594362Punjab election result: Congress' Kuldeep Singh Dhillon wins Barnala bypollCongress candidate Kuldeep Singh Dhillon won the Barnala assembly seat in the Punjab byelection, defeating AAP's Harinder Singh Dhaliwal by a margin of 2,147 votes. The bypolls, which took place on November 20, were necessitated by the election of the incumbent MLAs to the Lok Sabha.115593512Punjab bypolls: Clash breaks out between Congress, AAP in Dera Pathana villageA clash erupted between Congress and AAP workers at a Gurdaspur polling booth during the Dera Baba Nanak by-elections. Congress MP Sukhjinder Singh Randhawa urged voters to maintain peace, alleging an attempted booth capture by individuals he described as \"goons.\" By-elections are underway in four Punjab Assembly constituencies, including Dera Baba Nanak, with results expected on November 23rd.115476368
